Organised crime in India.description: Organised crime in India encompasses various criminal groups operating primarily in Mumbai and other urban centers, involved in smuggling, extortion, drug trafficking, and contract killings. The Mumbai underworld has been dominated by figures like Haji Mastan, Varadarajan Mudaliar, and Karim Lala since the 1940s. Indian organized crime extends internationally to countries such as Canada, the United States, and the United Kingdom. The groups are loosely structured and not strictly modeled after the Sicilian Mafia.

descriptions: Mumbai Underworld is listed as an ally of D-Company in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Dubai mafia, Mumbai Underworld

Mongrel Mob.description: The Mongrel Mob is a prominent organised crime group and prison gang based in New Zealand, founded in the 1960s by a group of mainly Pākehā youths from Wellington and Hawke's Bay. It is the largest gang in New Zealand with over thirty chapters, and also operates in Australia and Canada. The gang is known for its violent rivalry with Black Power and engages in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, extortion, and other crimes.

descriptions: Black Power was founded as a response to the Mongrel Mob, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was founded in response to the rival Mongrel Mob gang and white power associated gangs.

descriptions: Bloods are allied with Mongrel Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Black Guerrilla Family, Black P. Stones, Italian-American Mafia (in New York), Juggalos, Latin Kings, Mongrel Mob, People Nation, United Blood Nation, Vice Lords

descriptions: Blue Angels members forced a Mongrel Mob member to surrender his colors.
edges.evidence_quote: Four Blue Angels members ... pleaded guilty to threatening behaviour relating to an incident ... in which they forced a member of the Mongrel Mob to hand over his 'colours' at his home

descriptions: Club Deroes and three other clubs cooperated to violently eject the Mongrel Mob from Perth.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 these four gangs combined forces to violently eject the New Zealand-based street gang, the Mongrel Mob which was attempting to establish itself in Perth.

descriptions: Coffin Cheaters and three other clubs cooperated to violently eject the Mongrel Mob from Perth.

edges.evidence_quote: In June 1971, members of the Hells Angels, Highway 61, the Mongrel Mob and the Polynesian Panthers were involved in a large-scale brawl in Auckland, which resulted in numerous arrests.

Grape Street Watts Crips.description: The Grape Street Watts Crips is a set of the Crips gang based in the Watts neighborhood of Los Angeles, California. Active since the 1970s, it is known for a long-standing and violent rivalry with the Bounty Hunter Bloods. The gang operates in California, Tennessee, and New Jersey. A 2008 murder of a veteran member sparked a six-week war resulting in 26 wounded and 9 dead.

descriptions: Bounty Hunter Watts Bloods has a violent and long-lasting feud with Grape Street Watts Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: its longstanding rivalry with the Grape Street Watts Crips

descriptions: Grape Street Watts Crips is listed as a rival of Florencia 13 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 18th Street gang , 38th Street gang , Grape Street Watts Crips , Watts Varrio Grape Street 13 , Hat Gang Watts Crips , Huntington Park Locos , Playboys , East Side 13 , South Los 13

descriptions: Grape Street Watts Crips is listed as a rival of Fruit Town Piru in the infobox.

descriptions: Grape Street Watts Crips and Bounty Hunter Bloods are engaged in a violent, long-lasting feud.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang's rivalry with the Bounty Hunter Bloods has been described as being 'the most violent and long lasting feud between two gangs that are in the Watts area.'

descriptions: Grape Street Watts Crips is a subset of the larger Crips gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Grape Street Watts Crips is a set of the Crips gang

descriptions: East Coast Crips killed a Grape Street Watts Crips member, sparking a war between the two sets.
edges.evidence_quote: Brandon 'BL' Bullard, a veteran of the Grape Street Watts Crips, was murdered by the East Coast Crips in 2008. The murder of Bullard sparked a war that resulted in 26 wounded and 9 dead in the six weeks that followed.

descriptions: Grape Street Crips and PJ Watts Crips are feuding.
edges.evidence_quote: In Watts , the Grape Street Crips and the PJ Watts Crips have feuded so much that the PJ Watts Crips even teamed up with a local Blood set, the Bounty Hunter Bloods , to fight the Grape Street Crips.

Ku Klux Klan.description: The Ku Klux Klan is an American white supremacist and far-right hate group that has operated in three major iterations since the Reconstruction era. It uses violence and intimidation to target African Americans, Jews, Catholics, and immigrants, and is considered one of the earliest terrorist groups in the United States. The first Klan was founded in 1865 by former Confederate soldiers, the second in 1915, and a third wave emerged in the mid-20th century. Despite declines, affiliated groups remain active.

descriptions: The text speculates that the gang's name may be a coded reference to the Ku Klux Klan, but no actual relationship is asserted.
edges.evidence_quote: CBS News speculated that the name might be a coded reference to the Ku Klux Klan: K is the 11th letter of the alphabet, and there are 3 in 'KKK'.

descriptions: Infobox lists Ku Klux Klan as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Ku Klux Klan

descriptions: The Aryan Brotherhood lists the Ku Klux Klan as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Mafia estadounidense, Nazi Lowriders, Ku Klux Klan, Dirty White Boys, Public Enemy No. 1, Hells Angels MC, Vagos MC, Sureños, Mafia mexicana, cárteles mexicanos, Familia criminal Gambino, Familia criminal de Filadelfia, Mafia irlandesa.

descriptions: ARA leader Langan was ordained at a Klan-affiliated church.
edges.evidence_quote: Langan converted to Mormonism and became an ordained minister at what authorities describe as a Ku Klux Klan-affiliated church.

descriptions: Birger and the Shelton Brothers attacked the Klan in Herrin.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 1926, Charlie Birger and the Shelton Brothers joined forces to attack the remaining Klan leaders in Herrin, using Tommy guns and shotguns.

descriptions: The Charles Birger gang and Shelton Brothers Gang together fought the Ku Klux Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Shelton Brothers Gang and rival Charles Birger gang engaged in a war with the Ku Klux Klan that concluded with a shoot out at Herrin.

descriptions: The Grim Reapers have alleged ties to the Ku Klux Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: have alleged ties to the Ku Klux Klan (KKK)

descriptions: The ADL opposed the Klan's campaign against private schools.
edges.evidence_quote: The Jewish Anti-Defamation League of B'nai B'rith, founded in the early 20th century in response to attacks on Jewish Americans —including the lynching of Leo Frank in Atlanta—opposed the Klan's campaign to prohibit private schools

descriptions: The BSMPS was originally a chapter of the Ku Klux Klan in Fiji.
edges.evidence_quote: the chapter was renamed the "British Subjects' Mutual Protection Society" (BSMPS)

edges.target: European White Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
descriptions: European White Knights is a KKK-related group.
edges.evidence_quote: Another German KKK‑related group, the European White Knights of the Ku Klux Klan

descriptions: Imperial Klans of Brazil is a Klan-inspired group.
edges.evidence_quote: a group called itself the Imperial Klans of Brazil was shut down in 2003

descriptions: Senator Heflin compares the Klan to the Knights of Columbus in a speech, but no direct relationship is asserted.
edges.evidence_quote: What would you think if it sought to denounce the Knights of Columbus by the convention?

descriptions: Ku Klux Klan Kubano is a Klan-inspired group in Cuba.
edges.evidence_quote: an organization known as the Ku Klux Klan Kubano targeted West Indian migrant workers and Afro-Cubans

descriptions: The Klan opposed the Loyal Leagues, which mobilized Black voters.
edges.evidence_quote: Forrest said the Klan's primary opposition was to the Loyal Leagues

edges.target: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
descriptions: The NAACP campaigned against the Klan's activities.
edges.evidence_quote: The NAACP conducted public education campaigns about Klan activities and lobbied Congress against Klan abuses.

edges.target: National Socialist Knights of the Ku Klux Klan Deutschland
descriptions: National Socialist Knights of the Ku Klux Klan Deutschland is a KKK-related group.
edges.evidence_quote: German authorities conducted raids against a group called the National Socialist Knights of the Ku Klux Klan Deutschland

descriptions: The Klan and the National Socialist Party of America jointly attacked communist protesters.
edges.evidence_quote: On November 3, 1979, the Klan and the National Socialist Party of America killed five communist protesters in Greensboro, North Carolina, now known as the Greensboro massacre.

descriptions: Navi Clan modeled itself on the Ku Klux Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Navi Clan ("Holy Church of the White Race") was founded in Russia in 1996 and adopted Klan-style robes and rituals modeled on those of the Ku Klux Klan.

descriptions: The Pillar of Fire Church supported the Klan, but its leader denied membership.
edges.evidence_quote: It was supported by one small cult, the Pillar of Fire Church controlled by Bishop Alma Bridwell White, but she said she and her followers did not belong to the Klan.

descriptions: The Red Knights were formed to oppose the Klan and engaged in violent clashes.
edges.evidence_quote: The Red Knights, a militant group formed in opposition to the Klan, responded violently to Klan provocations on several occasions.

descriptions: The Red Shirts succeeded the first Klan as a similar paramilitary group.
edges.evidence_quote: After the Klan was suppressed, similar insurgent paramilitary groups emerged that were explicitly dedicated to suppressing Republican voting and removing Republicans from office. These included the White League, founded in Louisiana in 1874, and the Red Shirts.

descriptions: The Red Shirts succeeded the Klan as a paramilitary group targeting Black political leaders.
edges.evidence_quote: New groups of insurgents emerged in the mid-1870s, local paramilitary organizations such as the ... Red Shirts

descriptions: Ritter des Feurigen Kreuzes is a KKK-related group, indicating a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: a KKK-related group called Ritter des Feurigen Kreuzes ("Knights of the Fiery Cross") was founded in 1925 in Berlin

descriptions: Russian Ku Klux Klan is a group that imitates the Ku Klux Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: a group calling itself the Russian Ku Klux Klan staged a mock execution

descriptions: The first Klan borrowed elements of its initiation ceremonies from the Sons of Malta.
edges.evidence_quote: It began as a fraternal social club inspired in part by the then largely defunct Sons of Malta.

descriptions: Stormfront was launched by a former Klan grand wizard's ex-wife and Don Black, associated with the Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1995, Don Black and Chloë Hardin, the ex-wife of Ku Klux Klan grand wizard David Duke, launched a small bulletin board system (BBS) called Stormfront

edges.target: True Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
descriptions: The True Knights of the Ku Klux Klan is a group associated with the third Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 1997, FBI agents arrested four members of the True Knights of the Ku Klux Klan in Dallas for conspiracy to commit robbery and for conspiring to blow up a natural gas processing plant.

descriptions: The White League succeeded the first Klan as a similar paramilitary group.
edges.evidence_quote: After the Klan was suppressed, similar insurgent paramilitary groups emerged that were explicitly dedicated to suppressing Republican voting and removing Republicans from office. These included the White League, founded in Louisiana in 1874, and the Red Shirts.

descriptions: The White League succeeded the Klan as a paramilitary group targeting Black political leaders.
edges.evidence_quote: New groups of insurgents emerged in the mid-1870s, local paramilitary organizations such as the White League

edges.evidence_quote: han desarrollado vínculos con otras organizaciones como el Ku Klux Klan

edges.evidence_quote: The Shelton Brothers Gang and rival Charles Birger gang engaged in a war with the Ku Klux Klan that concluded with a shoot out at Herrin.

descriptions: Iannazzo is allied with Cannizzaro-Daponte.

Angiulo brothers.description: The Angiulo brothers were a leading Italian-American crime group based in Boston's North End from the 1960s until the mid-1980s. They operated as a street crew under the Patriarca crime family, controlling racketeering throughout Massachusetts. Their headquarters was the 'Dog House' convenience store. The group was dismantled after FBI bugging, aided by informants Whitey Bulger and Stephen Flemmi, led to indictments in 1983.

descriptions: Charlestown Mob competed with Angiulo brothers for control of rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: until Irish Mob groups such as the Winter Hill Gang and the Charlestown Mob decided to run the rackets in their own neighborhoods

descriptions: Angiulo brothers were members of the Patriarca crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: As "made men" in the Patriarca crime family, they were placed in control of the racketeering throughout Massachusetts

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang competed with Angiulo brothers for control of rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: until Irish Mob groups such as the Winter Hill Gang and the Charlestown Mob decided to run the rackets in their own neighborhoods

descriptions: The three clans have both conflicts and cooperative agreements for drug trafficking and extortion.
edges.evidence_quote: Nonostante queste tre batterie siano state spesso in conflitto, non sono mancati accordi con i quali sono stati costituiti assetti multipartecipativi ad esempio del traffico di droga o delle estorsioni per condividere e spartire il profitti.

descriptions: Marcello Catalano and Francesco Viscillo were close to the Piscopia clan, and their killing may relate to imposing decisions on other mafiosi, implying rivalry with Trisciuoglio-Prencipe.
edges.evidence_quote: Erano ritenuti vicini al clan Piscopia e una delle chiavi di lettura potrebbe essere il loro tentativo d'imporre decisioni ad altri mafiosi sui traffici di droga.

descriptions: Trisciuoglio-Prencipe-Tolonese are close allies of Moretti-Pellegrino.
edges.evidence_quote: i Trisciuoglio-Prencipe-Tolonese, stretti alleati dei Moretti-Pellegrino

descriptions: The attack on Trisciuoglio, Prencipe, and Piserchia was an attempt by a rival clan to eliminate the winning battery, referring to the Pellegrino group.
edges.evidence_quote: È il tentativo del clan rivale di spazzare i vertici della «batteria» vincente in quel momento.

descriptions: A member of Sinesi-Francavilla implicates a member of Trisciuoglio-Prencipe-Tolonese in a murder, indicating a connection but not specifying cooperation or conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Secondo le dichiarazioni di Patrizio Villani, sicario garganico al soldo della batteria Sinesi-Francavilla e condannato a 30 anni di reclusione per l'omicidio Tizzano, l'autore dell'omicidio Panunzio fu in realtà Federico Trisciuoglio, boss dell'omonima batteria Trisciuoglio-Prencipe-Tolonese e non Donato Delli Carri.

descriptions: Rivalry between Sinesi-Francavilla and Trisciuoglio-Prencipe groups led to the fourth mafia war.
edges.evidence_quote: Stando alla ricostruzione della polizia e della Direzione Investigativa Antimafia, furono le rivalità tra il gruppo di Roberto Sinesi e quello di Trisciuoglio-Prencipe a portare alla quarta guerra di mafia

descriptions: The three clans have both conflicts and cooperative agreements for drug trafficking and extortion.
edges.evidence_quote: Nonostante queste tre batterie siano state spesso in conflitto, non sono mancati accordi con i quali sono stati costituiti assetti multipartecipativi ad esempio del traffico di droga o delle estorsioni per condividere e spartire il profitti.

descriptions: The war ended with an alliance between Sinesi-Francavilla and Trisciuoglio-Prencipe to manage the funeral business together.
edges.evidence_quote: La guerra si concluse con un'alleanza tra i due gruppi consolidata al fine di gestire insieme il ricco business dei funerali.

descriptions: Morabito maintains strong ties with Criaco.
edges.evidence_quote: The Morabitos also maintain strong ties with the Maisano, Pansera, Versace, Zappia, Mollica, Criaco, and Scriva 'ndrine.

descriptions: Morabito has ties with Criaco.
edges.evidence_quote: Ma possono contare su legami con i Maisano, Pansera, Versace, Zappia, Mollica, Criaco e Scriva.

descriptions: ETA VII faction integrated into Euskadiko Ezkerra after accepting amnesty.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA VII accepted this partial amnesty granted by the now democratic Spanish government and integrated into the political party Euskadiko Ezkerra

Five Points Gang.description: The Five Points Gang was a criminal street gang based in the Five Points of Lower Manhattan, New York City, active from the 1890s to the 1920s. Founded by Paul Kelly (born Paolo Antonio Vaccarelli), it initially had Irish-American origins but later included many Italian-American members. The gang engaged in racketeering, extortion, drug trafficking, and other crimes. Notable members included Al Capone, Johnny Torrio, and Lucky Luciano.

descriptions: The Eastman Gang was at war with the Five Points Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was allied to the Eastman Gang who were at almost constant war with the Five Points and Humpty Jackson Gangs between the late 1890s and early 1900s

descriptions: Eastman Gang and Five Points Gang both succeeded the Whyos, and later warred over territory and political work.
edges.evidence_quote: Along with the increasingly Italian-American and Italian immigrant Five Points Gang under Italian-American Paolo Antonio Vaccarelli, best known by his pseudonym Paul Kelly , the Eastman gang succeeded the long dominant Whyos as the first non- Irish street gang to gain prominence in the underworld during the 1890s.

descriptions: The Eastman Gang competed with the Five Points Gang for control of New York's underworld.
edges.evidence_quote: Monk Eastman operated a powerful Jewish gang known as the Eastman Gang that competed with Italian and Irish gangs , notably Paul Kelly 's Five Points Gang , for control of New York City's underworld.

descriptions: The Eastman Gang competed with the Five Points Gang for control of New York City's underworld.
edges.evidence_quote: Monk Eastman operated a powerful Jewish gang known as the Eastman Gang that competed with Italian and Irish gangs, notably Paul Kelly's Five Points Gang, for control of New York City's underworld.

descriptions: Rival gang listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Batavia Street Gang

descriptions: Rival organization listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: New York Camorra

descriptions: Allied gang listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bugs and Meyer Gang

descriptions: Five Points Gang absorbed remaining members of the Dead Rabbits.
edges.evidence_quote: Kelly recruited the remaining members of other Five Points gangs, such as the Dead Rabbits and Whyos, into his growing gang.

descriptions: The two gangs fought over territory, engaging in open warfare.
edges.evidence_quote: The rivals disputed claims to a strip of territory of the Lower East Side of Manhattan.

descriptions: The Five Points Gang and Eastman Gang are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: This was in contrast to many of his fellow Jewish East Siders who predominantly joined the Five Points Gang's rival Eastman Gang led by its namesake Monk Eastman.

descriptions: The Five Points Gang was frequently in conflict with the Eastman Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Ellos estaban frecuentemente en conflicto con la Eastman Gang de la misma área.

descriptions: The Five Points Gang was often in conflict with the Eastmans.
edges.evidence_quote: They were often in conflict with the Jewish Eastmans of the same area.

descriptions: The Five Points Gang was strongly rivaled by the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Five Points Gang (led by Paul Kelly) would rise to prominence during the early 1900s, strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters, the Gopher Gang, and others during the period.

descriptions: Allied gang listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gopher Gang

descriptions: The Five Points Gang was strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters.
edges.evidence_quote: The Five Points Gang (led by Paul Kelly) would rise to prominence during the early 1900s, strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters, the Gopher Gang, and others during the period.

descriptions: Allied gang listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Hudson Dusters

descriptions: Rival gang listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Lenox Avenue Gang

descriptions: Allied Mafia family listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Morello crime family

descriptions: Tammany Hall supported the Five Points Gang politically.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Tammany Hall

descriptions: Rival gang listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: White Hand Gang

descriptions: Five Points Gang absorbed remaining members of the Whyos.
edges.evidence_quote: Kelly recruited the remaining members of other Five Points gangs, such as the Dead Rabbits and Whyos, into his growing gang.

descriptions: The Whyos included reconstituted members of previous Five Points area gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Another late 19th century New York gang was the Jewish Eastman Gang. Meanwhile, Chinese immigrants formed tongs, which were highly structured gangs involved in gambling and drug trafficking. These tongs were matched in strength by an emerging Italian organized crime network that became the American Mafia.

descriptions: Allied gang listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Yakey Yakes

descriptions: The Gas House Gang was absorbed by the Five Points Gang in 1910.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang continued to control the district for over two decades until it was eventually absorbed by the Five Points Gang in 1910.

descriptions: Five Points Gang rose to prominence as the Whyos declined, but no direct relationship stated.
edges.evidence_quote: As Monk Eastman and the Five Points Gang came to prominence in the mid-1890s

descriptions: Karamojong Boys is a splinter group of Baghdad Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Other splinter groups include ChinaSquadNyalenda Base, Chief Squad, Nyamasaria Massive, Kenda Kenda, Kondele Baghdad for Peace, Karamojong Boys, Saba Saba, Artur Margaryan, Kebago, and American Marines.

Bacon Brothers.description: The Bacon brothers are a trio of gangsters from Abbotsford, British Columbia, involved in firearms and drug trafficking, and implicated in multiple homicides in the Fraser Valley and Greater Vancouver area. The group consists of Jonathan, Jarrod, and Jamie Bacon. Jonathan was murdered in 2011. They are associated with the United Nations gang.

descriptions: Jamie Bacon condemned the Brothers' Keepers gang, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Bacon condemned the Wolfpack Alliance together with the Indo-Canadian Brothers' Keepers gang in racist and homophobic language.

descriptions: The Bacon Brothers organization has links with the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: has links with a notorious criminal organization, an apparent reference to the Hells Angels

descriptions: The Bacon brothers partnered with the Red Scorpions and later took leadership control.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2006, the brothers partnered with the Red Scorpions gang to help them compete with the United Nations gang.

descriptions: The Bacon Brothers were lured to join the Red Scorpions.
edges.evidence_quote: luring the Bacon Brothers away from the increasingly independent and unreliable UN to the more docile and malleable Red Scorpions

descriptions: Jarrod Bacon is identified as a member of both the Red Scorpions and the Bacon Brothers organization.
edges.evidence_quote: File information reveals that you are identified as a member of the Red Scorpions and the Bacon Brothers organization

descriptions: The Bacon brothers worked with the United Nations gang, though they held contempt for its leader.
edges.evidence_quote: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) believed the Bacon brothers were working with the United Nations gang.

descriptions: The Bacon Brothers were previously associated with the United Nations gang before being lured away.
edges.evidence_quote: luring the Bacon Brothers away from the increasingly independent and unreliable UN

descriptions: Jamie Bacon condemned the Wolfpack Alliance, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Bacon condemned the Wolfpack Alliance together with the Indo-Canadian Brothers' Keepers gang in racist and homophobic language.

descriptions: Fresh Off the Boat Killers members were recruited to assassinate the Bacon brothers.
edges.evidence_quote: In early 2008, Clayton Roueche recruited two members of Calgary's Fresh Off the Boat Killers gang, namely Billy Ly and Troy Tran, to come to the Lower Mainland to kill the Bacon brothers.

descriptions: Jamie Bacon was a member of the Red Scorpions gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Jamie did not resist arrest and was led into a police cruiser wearing his Red Scorpions hoodie and track pants.

descriptions: The United Nations gang, led by Tilli-Choli, actively hunted and opposed the Bacon brothers.
edges.evidence_quote: Tilli-Choli, who became the most tenacious opponent of the Bacon brothers, was known for the 'human safaris' as he would spend hours cruising the Lower Mainland in his automobile looking for the Bacon brothers and other members of the Red Scorpions gang.

descriptions: Jonathan Bacon was a founding member of the Wolfpack Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Jonathan Bacon together with Larry Amero of the Hells Angels and James Riach and Randy Naicker of the Independent Soldiers formed a group called the Wolfpack Alliance in 2010.

descriptions: Saudi Arabia funded Hamas operations early on.
edges.evidence_quote: Hamas enjoyed close relations with Saudi Arabia in its early years. Saudi Arabia funded most of its operations from 2000 to 2004, but reduced its support due to US pressure.

edges.evidence_quote: Fragmentación ... - La Oficina en Aguascalientes .

descriptions: A dronero from Chapo Isidro's faction attacked a Gente del Guano vehicle, indicating conflict between MZ/MF-aligned groups.

descriptions: Gente del Guano is aligned with MZ/MF.
edges.evidence_quote: On 6 May 2025, armed men belonging to "Gente Del Guano" seen patrolling in Badiraguato, Sinaloa.

Tottenham Mandem.description: Organised street gang based in Tottenham, North London, founded in the 1970s on the Broadwater Farm estate. Active in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, armed robbery, kidnapping, and contract killing. One of the largest gangs in North London during the 1990s, controlling much of the local drug markets. Declined in the early 2000s, splintering into groups like NPK and Star Gang.

descriptions: Tottenham Mandem is an ally of Ghetto Boys per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Tottenham Mandem

descriptions: Shower became associated with Tottenham Mandem to form Shower Syndicate.
edges.evidence_quote: later became associated with the Tottenham Mandem , collectively calling themselves "Shower Syndicate"

descriptions: Star Gang is an offshoot group from Tottenham Mandem.
edges.evidence_quote: They were both originally rappers in Star Gang, a road rap group from the 2000s and an offshoot group from the Tottenham Mandem gang.

descriptions: Infobox lists Broadwater Farm Posse as ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Broadwater Farm Posse

descriptions: Tottenham Mandem derived from Broadwater Farm Posse.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang is a derivative of another local gang known as the Broadwater Farm Posse, formed in the 1970s by Black British youth of West Indian origins.

descriptions: Burger Bar Boys listed as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Broadwater Farm Posse, Burger Bar Boys, Causeway Gang, Edmonton Firm (also known as The Firm, Edmonton Mandem, or EMD), Star Gang

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Burger Bar Boys

descriptions: BWF formed from younger TMD members associating with London Bloods.

descriptions: Chestnut Estate is a split-off group from TMD.
edges.evidence_quote: Some of the Tottenham split-offs from TMD were ... Chestnut Estate (CE, also known as Black Gang)

descriptions: Farm Mandem is a collective term for people from Broadwater Farm, including TMD members.
edges.evidence_quote: Collectively, everyone from the Broadwater Farm estate began to be referred to as 'Farm Mandem' (FMD).

descriptions: Farm Mandem is a collective term for fragmentations that still had links to older TMD members.
edges.evidence_quote: Collectively, some of the fragmentations were all known as 'Farm Mandem' (FMD), and they still had links to older TMD members.

descriptions: TMD members became associated with Ghetto Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: In the mid-late 2000s, members of TMD became associated with the Ghetto Boys

descriptions: TMD and Hackney Boys were in a violent conflict with tit-for-tat killings.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1997, TMD had a conflict with another local gang known as the Hackney Boys (also known as Hackney Mandem, or HMD). The conflict resulted in a number of tit-for-tat killings.

Manson Family.description: A commune, gang, and cult led by Charles Manson, active in California in the late 1960s and early 1970s. The group consisted of approximately 100 followers who lived an unconventional lifestyle and used psychoactive drugs. They committed at least nine murders, including the Tate-LaBianca murders, and other crimes. The group dissolved after Manson and other members were arrested.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood had a brief connection with Manson Family, attempted to join forces, but ended due to offense over Sharon Tate murder.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1970s, the Aryan Brotherhood had a connection with Charles Manson and the Manson Family. Several members of the Manson Family were in prison at the time, and they attempted to join forces. However, the relationship did not last long as the Aryan Brotherhood took offense at the murder of pregnant actress Sharon Tate.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood had a connection with the Manson Family.
edges.evidence_quote: A principios de la década de 1970, la Hermandad Aria tenía una conexión con Charles Manson y la Familia Manson.

descriptions: Manson found temporary acceptance from the Aryan Brotherhood in prison, but his role was submissive.
edges.evidence_quote: Though he found temporary acceptance from the Aryan Brotherhood , his role was submissive to a sexually aggressive member of the group at San Quentin .

descriptions: Manson Family feared retaliation from the Black Panthers after shooting Bernard Crowe.
edges.evidence_quote: Manson concluded he had been and expected retaliation from the Panthers.

descriptions: Manson Family hired Straight Satans members for security at Spahn Ranch.
edges.evidence_quote: Manson brought in members of the Straight Satans Motorcycle Club to act as security.

descriptions: The Straight Satans had a close association with the Manson Family, with members visiting Spahn Ranch and providing weapons.
edges.evidence_quote: The Straight Satans are perhaps most infamous for their connection to the Manson Family cult.
